questions.question: Which of the following is the correct sequence to create a new file in WordPad on a Windows computer?
How can you resize a window using its corners or borders in Windows?
questions.option_1: Click the middle of the window and press Ctrl.
questions.option_2: Place the mouse pointer over a border or corner until it turns into a double-headed arrow, then drag.
questions.option_3: Drag the title bar to change the size.
questions.option_4: Press Alt + Enter to auto-resize the window.

questions.question: What does CPU stand for in computer systems?
Which memory is non-volatile, meaning it retains data even after the computer is turned off?
Which key is used to rename a file or folder in Windows?
What is the function of the Alt + F4 shortcut?
questions.option_4: Closes the current window or application

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the role of the Control Unit within the Central Processing Unit?
questions.option_1: It stores data and instructions temporarily during processing.
questions.option_2: It performs all mathematical and logical calculations.
questions.option_3: It displays the processed output to the user.
questions.option_4: It directs and coordinates the activities of all components of the computer.

questions.question: Which of the following steps allows you to create a new folder on the desktop in Windows?
What is the correct way to copy a file or folder using the Drag and Drop method?
questions.option_1: Select the file in the right pane → Drag it to a folder in the left pane → Drop it there
questions.option_2: Double-click the file → Drag it back into the same folder
questions.option_3: Select the file → Press Ctrl + C → Drop it into the Recycle Bin
questions.option_4: Right-click the file → Drag it to the desktop → Press Delete

questions.question: How can you permanently delete a file in MS Windows so that it DOES NOT go to the Recycle Bin?
questions.option_1: Right-click on the file and press 'Shift + Delete'
questions.option_2: Right-click on the file and Press Ctrl + D
questions.option_3: Drag the file to the Recycle Bin
questions.option_4: Right-click on the file and select 'Delete'

questions.question: Which Windows feature helps to quickly locate a specific file in a large folder?
What is the primary function of the Taskbar in Windows7?
questions.option_2: To show open programs and allow quick access to them

questions.question: What is the function of the Taskbar in the Windows operating system?
questions.option_2: Displays currently running applications and allows switching between them

questions.question: Which of the following devices performs optical character recognition (OCR)?
Which of the following combinations of shortcut keys and functions is correctly matched?
questions.option_1: Ctrl + V – Finds a specific word in the document
questions.option_2: Ctrl + C – Copies content to the clipboard
questions.option_3: Ctrl + F – Switches between open tabs
questions.option_4: Ctrl + Tab – Copies the selected text

questions.question: Which of the following methods is used to rename a file or folder in MS Windows?
questions.option_1: Double-click the file to open it

questions.question: What does the abbreviation ASCII stand for?
questions.option_1: Automated System Code for International Interfacing
questions.option_2: Advanced Standard Communication for Information Integration
questions.option_3: American Standard Code for Information Interchange
questions.option_4: American Standard Code for Internet Information

questions.question: What does the abbreviation EBCDIC stand for?
questions.option_1: Electronic Binary Code for Data Interchange Communication
questions.option_2: Extended Binary Coded Decimal Interchange Code
questions.option_3: Encoded Binary Character Data Interchange Code
questions.option_4: Extended Binary Code for Digital Information Communication

questions.question: Which of these elements is NOT part of the standard Windows 7 desktop?
Which of the following options is used to change the system settings in MS Windows?
Which of the following is a primary memory of a computer?
What does the abbreviation 'MAN' stand for in computer networking?
Which of the following is a good way to organise files in Windows?
questions.option_1: Create separate folders for different types of files
questions.option_2: Keep files with different subjects in one folder
questions.option_3: Store all files on the desktop
questions.option_4: Rename all files with random numbers

questions.question: Which statement best differentiates ROM from RAM in terms of volatility and usage?
questions.option_1: RAM is permanent; ROM is temporary.
questions.option_2: RAM is volatile and stores temporary data; ROM is non-volatile and stores boot instructions.
questions.option_3: ROM is volatile and used for running programs; RAM is non-volatile and stores firmware.
questions.option_4: ROM is used for multitasking; RAM is used for storing BIOS.
